title: dodonaea serratifolia
intro: Dodonaea serratifolia, commonly known as the sawbush or hop-bush, is a shrub-like plant native to eastern Australia, particularly in the coastal regions of New South Wales and Queensland. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dodonaea serratifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
